title: Previewing Changes with kubectl diff
description:
## Previewing Changes with `kubectl diff`

`kubectl diff` compares a local manifest file against the **live state** of a resource in the cluster, showing exactly what would change if you applied it — without actually making any changes. This is a safe way to review updates before rolling them out.

**Your task:**

A Deployment named `diffme` is running with 1 replica and image `nginx:1.24`.

1. Write a updated manifest for the same Deployment with **3 replicas** and image `nginx:1.25` to `/tmp/diffme-updated.yaml`
2. Run `kubectl diff` against it to preview the changes
3. Then **apply** the updated manifest to make the changes live

```bash
# Preview changes:
kubectl diff -f /tmp/diffme-updated.yaml

# Apply changes:
kubectl apply -f /tmp/diffme-updated.yaml
```

hints.title: Write the updated manifest
hints.body: Create a YAML file with the updated replicas and image values.
hints.command:
cat <<EOF > /tmp/diffme-updated.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: diffme
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: diffme
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: diffme
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:1.25
EOF

hints.title: Run kubectl diff
hints.body: kubectl diff exits with code 1 if there are differences (expected), code 0 if nothing changed.

hints.title: Apply the manifest
hints.body: Once you have reviewed the diff output, apply the manifest to update the live cluster state.
